steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three deities in an appositive phrase opened by an em dash after "pantheon—including." The list runs: Apollo; Dionysus; and the god of constellations, ______. The blank must close the parenthetical phrase so the main clause ("may have been historical figures...") can resume.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Crius;': a semicolon signals another list item or clause is coming — it does not close the em-dash parenthetical. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Crius—': a matching em dash closes the appositive phrase opened earlier, allowing the main clause to continue correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Crius,': a comma is too weak to close a parenthetical opened by an em dash; the structure stays open. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Crius': no punctuation leaves the appositive phrase unclosed, fusing it into the main clause. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
